title: Design System Documentation Standard (DSDS) v0.1
description: A machine-readable format for design system documentation covering components, design tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and patterns. A DSDS document contains one or more documentation groups — named collections of typed entities. Each entity carries a `kind` discriminator identifying it as a component, token, token-group, theme, style, or pattern. Entity schemas are in entities/. Guideline schemas are in guidelines/. Common schemas are in common/.

$schema.description: URI reference to the DSDS JSON Schema for validation.

dsdsVersion.description: The DSDS spec version this document conforms to.

extends.description: Declares that this DSDS document inherits from another DSDS document — typically a core design system. The base document provides the foundational entities. This document layers on top, adding or extending entities. Enables systems-of-systems architectures where product- or brand-specific systems inherit from a shared core.

purpose.description: The purpose of the design system as a whole — why it exists, what problems it solves, and who it serves. Helps stakeholders, new team members, and external consumers understand the system's intent before exploring individual components or tokens.

description.description: A high-level description of the design system's purpose and the problems it solves.

useCases.description: Scenario-driven guidance describing when this design system is or is not the right choice. Positive cases describe when the system should be adopted. Negative cases describe when a different approach fits better, and why.

bestPractices.description: System-level best practices that apply across the entire design system — broad guidance for teams adopting, contributing to, or consuming the system. These are not component-specific rules (those live in each entity's guidelines array) but cross-cutting principles like 'always use semantic tokens instead of raw values' or 'test all components at 200% zoom'. Each entry is a self-contained rule paired with a rationale.

documentation.description: One or more documentation groups. Each group is a named collection of typed entities — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns. Multiple groups let a single DSDS file organize entities into logical sections (e.g., one group for foundations, another for components).

documentationGroup.description: A named collection of design system documentation entities. Contains a single `items` array of typed entities — components, tokens, token groups, themes, styles, and/or patterns mixed in any order. Each entity is identified by its `kind` discriminator. At least one entity must be present.

name.description: Human-readable name of the collection (e.g., 'Acme Design System', 'Color Tokens', 'Button Documentation').

items.description: The entities in this documentation group. Each item is a typed entity whose `kind` property determines which schema applies. Different entity types can be mixed in the same array. Ordering is significant: tools SHOULD preserve it for display. Authors MAY organize entities in whatever order best serves their documentation.
